evidence_excerpt: The child is now 3 years old, has light perception vision, severe developmental delay, and microcephaly.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Persistent profound visual impairment (light perception only) with severe global developmental delay and microcephaly.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Corneal edema developed with IOPs of 35 mmHg (right) and 45 mmHg (left)... settled on cessation of steroid drops.
Elevated IOP and corneal edema settled on cessation of steroid drops.
Contact lens wear was instituted within days of surgery, but no evidence of vision appeared.
By 9 months, severe global developmental delay and microcephaly (head circumference 43 cm, 0.4th centile).
At age 3, persistent light perception vision only, severe developmental delay, and microcephaly.

notes_on_uncertainty: Data extracted from the detailed case report of Patient 2 in a case series. The diagnosis was a working diagnosis made at the initial eye clinic visit prior to cataract surgery. Long-term follow-up is limited to age 3; outcomes beyond this are not documented.
